title: Preoperative Care and Teaching
subtitle: The nurse prepares a patient for surgery — confirming the surgeon-obtained consent before sedation, NPO status, the preop checklist, and teaching coughing, deep breathing, splinting, and early ambulation.

patient.name: Preoperative Care
patient.admitDate: The nurse prepares a patient for surgery with consent verification, the preop checklist, and preoperative teaching.

nursesNote: The nurse prepares the patient for surgery. The surgeon explains the procedure, risks, benefits, and alternatives and obtains the informed consent; the nurse witnesses the signature, confirms the patient understands, and notifies the surgeon if the patient has questions or seems unsure — and the consent must be signed before any sedating preop medication. The nurse verifies NPO status to reduce aspiration risk, completes the preop checklist (baseline vitals, labs, allergies, ID band), and has the patient remove dentures, jewelry, contacts, and nail polish. The patient voids before transfer, and ordered preop medications and VTE prophylaxis (sequential compression devices or stockings) are applied. Preoperative teaching covers coughing and deep breathing, incentive spirometer use, splinting the incision, early ambulation, and leg exercises to prevent postoperative pneumonia, atelectasis, and blood clots.
providerOrders: Confirm the surgeon-obtained consent is signed before sedation
Verify NPO status and complete the preoperative checklist
Have the patient remove jewelry, dentures, and nail polish and void
Apply VTE prophylaxis and give preop medications as ordered
Teach coughing, deep breathing, splinting, and early ambulation

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What is true about preoperative care?

opts.text: The surgeon, not the nurse, explains the procedure and obtains consent
The nurse witnesses the consent signature and confirms understanding
NPO status before surgery reduces the risk of aspiration
Consent can be signed after preop sedation is given
Dentures, jewelry, and nail polish are removed before surgery
The patient should void before transfer to the OR
If the patient doesn't understand the procedure, notify the surgeon
Teaching coughing, deep breathing, and spirometry prevents complications
Anti-embolism measures help prevent postoperative blood clots
Preop teaching should be skipped to avoid worrying the patient
Splinting the incision reduces pain when coughing
Baseline vital signs and labs are documented before surgery

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: Order preparing the patient the morning of surgery

opts.text: Verify the signed consent and confirm NPO status
Take baseline vitals and complete the preop checklist
Have the patient remove jewelry, dentures, and nail polish
Have the patient void and don a gown with an ID band
Give preop medications as ordered and transfer to the OR
Explaining the procedure, risks, and alternatives
Witnessing the patient's signature
Confirming the patient understands and answering basic questions
Obtaining the informed consent
Verifying the form is signed before sedation
Describing the specific risks and benefits of the operation
Notifying the surgeon when the patient has new questions
Discussing the surgical technique and expected outcomes

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: Before surgery the patient says they're unsure of the procedure
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Stop and do not give the preop sedative yet
Assess what the patient understands
Notify the surgeon of the patient's questions
Allow the surgeon to clarify and answer
Confirm understanding before proceeding
Confirming the consent is signed before sedation
Giving a sedative, then having the patient sign consent
Verifying NPO status before surgery
Letting the patient keep dentures and rings to the OR
Teaching incentive spirometry and incision splinting
Skipping preop teaching to save time
Notifying the surgeon when the patient is unsure
Applying sequential compression devices as ordered

reference: Aligned to the NCSBN NCLEX Test Plan client-need category: Reduction of Risk Potential, using the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model. Written from standard nursing references. Pending faculty review.
